key_concepts: When outliers are valid events, deleting them loses data, but keeping them as-is can skew sensitive models (like linear regression).
Robust transformations reduce the magnitude and influence of outliers without destroying the extreme signal entirely.
Techniques include capping/winsorizing (setting extreme values to a specified percentile boundary) and robust scaling (scaling based on interquartile ranges rather than mean/variance).
Logarithmic or power transformations can also compress the long tails of skewed distributions, naturally reigning in outliers.

definitions.definition: Limiting extreme values in the statistical data to reduce the effect of possibly spurious outliers.
Scaling features using statistics that are robust to outliers, typically the median and interquartile range.

examples: Capping all incomes above the 99th percentile to match the exact value of the 99th percentile.
Applying a log transform to heavily skewed transaction amounts so that $1,000,000 becomes closer in scale to $10,000.
exclusions: Repeating the exact formulas for standard scaling vs robust scaling (already in Chapter 3).
